Belgium dbt Meetup #14

Hosted by Sam D.

Belgium dbt Meetup
4.7
54 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue: BeCentral, Cantersteen 12, 1000 Brussels (same building as the Brussels Central Station)
🤝Organizers: Sam Debruyn
🅿️ Interparking Albertina is at 5min walking distance
🚂 It is literally inside Belgium's best connected train station

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come. Reach out - send a message in #local-belgium on Slack - if you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

📝Agenda:

18h00: welcome with food & drinks
18h45: start presentations
20h00: networking & drinks

🗣️Presentations:

Scaling dbt (core) Infrastructure at Lighthouse
This session will be about creating standardized dbt components (internal dbt package, reusable CICD template, ...) to scale the use of dbt core across an entire organisation with many repositories.

by Marthe Van Den Hende, Data Platform Engineer at Lighthouse

*Marthe is a Data Platform Engineer at Lighthouse with hands-on experience implementing dbt in SaaS companies, focusing on scalability. Before Lighthouse, she worked as a Data Engineer at Markmi.ai.*

Accelerating Data Engineering with dbt MCP Server
In this session, I’ll introduce a more experimental approach to data engineering using a dbt MCP server. We’ll explore how this setup enhances developer productivity by enabling smarter workflows, automation, and tighter integration between dbt and external tools.

I’ll share early learnings from real-world usage, including how MCP can streamline model development, improve context awareness, and reduce friction in day-to-day analytics engineering tasks. This session is ideal for those curious about the next evolution of dbt workflows and how emerging tooling can augment traditional SQL-based development.

by Vladyslav Ishchenko, Analytics Architect at Element61

Vladyslav holds an MBA from KU Leuven specializing in Business Information Management and brings strong multicultural experience from living in Japan, Vietnam, and South Korea. He specializes in Microsoft Azure cloud technologies, data engineering, and analytics, with expertise in SQL, Python, Synapse, Databricks, and Power BI, and holds multiple Azure certifications. At element61, he designs large-scale data pipelines and delivers data-driven solutions that unlock business value for customers.

We are always looking for speakers!
To submit a session for one of the next meetups, please use our Sessionize page.
Are you in doubt if you're ready to give a talk? Check out dbt Labs's guide on how to deliver a fantastic presentation!

🤝 For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-belgium channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/)!

Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

The 12th edition of the Belgium dbt Meetup will take place in Louvain-La-Neuve on November 5th.

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host & Catering Provider: IBA Headquarters, Chem. du Cyclotron 3, 1348 Ottignies-Louvain-la-Neuve
🅿️ We have plenty of car parking space available.
🚂 The venue is at walking distance (5 minutes) from the train station of Louvain-La-Neuve.
🤝Organizers: Sam Debruyn & Kaan Kizilirmak

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come. Reach out - send a message in #local-belgium on Slack - if you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

📝Agenda:

18h00: welcome with food & drinks
18h45: start presentations
20h15: networking & drinks

🗣️Presentations:

Using dbt to manage your data warehouse
In this twofold session, I'll cover how we've used dbt to bring order in heaps of SQL statements used to manage a datawarehouse. I'd like to share how dbt made our team more efficient and our data warehouse more resilient. Secondly, I'll highlight why dbt enabled a way forward on supporting low-code applications: by leveraging our data warehouse as a backend.
I'll dive into systemic design, application architecture & data modelling. Tools/tech covered will be SQL, Trino, Outsystems, GIT, Airflow and of course dbt!
Expect practical insights, architectural patterns, and lessons learned from a real-world implementation.

by Kobe Thuwis, Data Engineer @ Infofarm
Turning coffee into code ☕
I'm a passionate data engineer who loves diving into technical challenges and building robust, scalable solutions.

From chaos to control - how we scaled dbt for 150 analysts and engineers
Managing dbt for 150 analytics engineers meant evolving from fragmented dbt Core projects to unified standards, migrating to dbt Cloud. We solved security risks and inconsistent practices through standardization and centralized workflows, while maintaining our Airflow orchestration. Challenges remain in balancing governance with analyst autonomy at scale.

by Mathias Lavaert, Principal Data Engineer @ DPG Media
Mathias has a decade of experience working on several data-intensive projects in various roles, ranging from engineer to enterprise data architect. Currently he helps DPG Media to transition to a data mesh architecture as a Principal Data Engineer. Mathias lives on the outskirts of Brussels with his wife. Together they're building a small farm specialized in breeding Herdwick sheep.

Fusion360
Let's explore the capabilities of dbt's brand-new engine: Fusion.
We’ll explore it from different angles, such as performance, functionalities, and how it transforms the developer experience.

by Kaan Kizilirmak, Data Engineer at IBA
A data engineer with an analytical focus. My career trajectory began in mechatronics and automation, transitioned towards cloud data engineering.

The 11th edition of the Belgium dbt Meetup will take place in Antwerp on June 24th.

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host & Catering Provider: Port of Antwerp-Bruges, Zaha Hadidplein 1, 2030 Antwerp
🤝Organizers: Sam Debruyn & Cedric Raeymaeckers
🚆 Getting there: Parking is available under the building (private parking), with the entrance located on Mexicostraat. When entering the parking, you can indicate that you are attending the dbt meetup, and you will be granted access.

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come. Reach out - send a message in #local-belgium on Slack - if you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

📝Agenda:

18h00: welcome with food & drinks
18h45: start presentations
20h30: networking & drinks

🗣️Presentations:

One dbt repo, two targets: parallel dbt deployments on Azure SQL & Databricks
We’ll start with a walkthrough of the technical setup of dbt at the Port of Antwerp-Bruges, in the context of a migration to Databricks. Then we'll dive into how we handle deploying dbt to multiple targets for the duration of the migration. Finally we'll compare both environments with insights from an analytics engineering perspective.
by Emiel Ackermann, Technical Lead at Port of Antwerp-Bruges & Nicolas Jonckheere, Lead Analytics Engineer at Datashift

A strategic approach to testing with Data Products in dbt
With data teams' growing ambition to build business automation, AI systems, or customer-facing products, we must shift our mindset about data quality. Mechanically applied testing will not be enough; we need a more robust strategy similar to software engineering. In this talk, I outline a new approach to data testing and observability anchored in the ‘Data Products’ concept and walk through the practical implementation of a production-grade analytics system with dbt as the backbone. The learnings will apply to data practitioners using dbt whether they're just getting started or working in a large enterprise.
by Mikkel Dengsøe, Co-Founder of SYNQ
Mikkel is the co-founder of Synq. Prior to Synq, he was leading data across operations and financial crime at UK fintech Monzo. Before that, he spent five years at Google working in data roles.

For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs
In this meetup, we’ll dive into the latest tools and ideas we’ve been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did.
This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.
Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.
by Bart van Delft, Solutions Architect, dbt Labs
Bart is from the Netherlands. With a passion for data dating back to 2014 when he wrote his master's thesis about the topic, Bart has since garnered experience in the field at companies like Snowflake and Oracle.

to dbt or not to dbt
dbt has become king in the world of data transformations, but is it here to stay? We’ll explore how dbt’s competitors are trying to distinguish themselves, and how dbt is responding to the competition.

by Michiel De Muynck, Senior Data Engineer @ Dataminded
Michiel did a PhD and postdoc in computer science at Ghent University. In 2022, Michiel joined Datamindeld, where he has both used dbt at clients as well as taught dbt courses for Dataminded Academy.

dbt @ Immoscoop
Presentation on how our data platform changed before and after the introduction of dbt. We’ll explore how dbt helped us manage our data warehouse and drive Immoscoop’s strategy, touching on model types, materializations, semantic layers, and more.

by Brecht Van Buggenhout, Data Analyst @ Immoscoop
Brecht studied in the United States for six years and earned a Master’s in Business Analytics from the University of Wisconsin–Madison. After graduating, he started as a Data Analyst at Immoscoop, where he experienced the complete transformation of their data platform from day one. He now serves as a Team Lead, overseeing all data-related activities.

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Gee, stop building into production!
In this talk I will give an overview of how you can bring the Write-Audit-Publish pattern from software engineering to your dbt project thanks to the neat features of modern data warehouses. I will also tap on the benefits and costs as well as the limitations of implementing these solutions.

Sonny Nguyen
Data Engineer at POLITICO
A data folk @ POLITICO who creates scalable pipelines and assists businesses in adopting modern data stack technologies. I have years of profound experience implementing solutions with dbt and the Modern Data Stack in the venture, manufacturing, and fashion retail industry.

dbt semantic layer
We will start by exploring the concepts of a semantic layer and why it has been gaining traction recently. We will talk why it is a foundation pillar for LLM and finish with a hands-on demo.

Charles Verleyen
Lead architect at Astrafy
I am a 34 years old data engineer and have been working for the last 10 years in different industry before founding my company recently with a focus on providing modern data services.

Nawfel Bacha
Data Engineer at Astrafy
Data Architecture & Data Modeling

Analytics Engineering in Data Team Dynamics
The role of Analytics Engineering has gained prominence in the job market, often associated with the use of dbt. However, Analytics Engineers have been integral to data teams even before dbt popularized it.
This presentation aims to clarify the role of an Analytics Engineer within a data team. It delves into the challenges faced by Data Engineers, Data Analysts, and Data Scientists, providing insights into the frustrations encountered in these roles. The discussion focuses on how an Analytics Engineer can serve as a valuable support in addressing these challenges. Furthermore, it explores the role of an Analytics Engineer in enabling a more efficient Data Mesh and highlights the synergies with the modern data stack.
Additionally, this presentation explores various use cases and detailing projects where an Analytics Engineer has directly addressed data challenges by closely working with Data Engineers and business stakeholders.

Maryam Ilyas
Analytics Engineer at Dataroots
Maryam started as a data analyst, quickly she encountered the challenges inherent in the field answering business stakeholder. Gradually, she transitioned into the role of an Analytics Engineer.

📝Agenda:

17h45: welcome with food & drinks
18h30: start presentations
20h30: networking & drinks

🗣️Presentations:

Streamlining unit testing in dbt: Increasing coverage with the least amount of effort
Unit testing of data transformations has long been a cumbersome process. In this talk I'll discuss the goals (and frustrations) we had that led us to look for the most streamlined way to implement unit tests, as well as how we got to our current approach.

Wim Deca
Data Architect at Digital Hive
Wim has been working with data since the beginning of the 2010s and is one of the driving forces behind Digital Hive's Data Unit, a small consulting boutique shop that specializes in engineering and architecture expertise for the modern data stack.

Reigniting Data Modeling: Sensible Strategies for the 2020s and Beyond
This talk covers theoretical and practical learnings from Niko's journey to productize data modeling best practices into performant dbt code, across major data warehouses. In this talk Niko will cover long forgotten data modeling best practices, as well as design you can apply to your own dbt projects. Be prepared: This is not going to be jaffle shop but instead we’ll dive to the deep end with incrementality, temporality and event ledgers. All whilst not blowing up your compute costs. Niko will also share practical learnings on how to build agile data processes in DWH centric setup.

Niko Korvenlaita
CTO, Co-Founder at reconfigured
Niko Korvenlaita, a seasoned data pro for a decade, with experience ranging from Excel to YouTube scale data setups. Currently building reconfigured - a data modeling platform that enables data pros to convert business logic into production-grade dbt code.

Using dbt to lower BigQuery costs
In this session I'd like to share how I used dbt in different ways to lower BigQuery costs in various projects. This includes:
- Cost guardrail techniques with max_bytes_billed, batch jobs and max_concurrency
- Cost observability with job labels & smart usage of information_schemas
- Cost lowering with partitioning, clustering and materialized views

Rik Pauwels
Data Architect at Devoteam G Cloud
I'm a Data Architect at Devoteam G Cloud with a specialisation in Data Warehouses & Customer Data Platforms on BigQuery.
Over the past years I helped multiple big organisations in setting up their own data platform on Google Cloud, this included training, the creation of new pipelines and extensive reviews with respect to cost and security.
Next to that I'm also the proud possessor of all Google Cloud Certificates as well as being recognized as an Google Cloud Authorized Trainer.

### Behind the scenes: contributing to dbt-core and its adapters

In this talk, we will go through the inner workings of dbt. How does it do its magic? What better way to learn how dbt works than by contributing to it yourself? We'll dig deeper into how dbt is built and how you can take a look under hood yourself by making your first contributions.

Sam Debruyn - tech lead data & cloud at dataroots
Sam is tech lead of dataroots’s data & cloud unit. He is passionate about data analytics and open-source software. dbt is at the crossroads of where his passions meet. Sam is an early adopter of dbt, the Belgian dbt community leader, top dbt contributor, receiver of the dbt Community Award, and dbt community spotlight member. Short to say, he really seems to know a lot about dbt and is eager to share this knowledge!

### A Journey to Data Culture

Many companies call themselves data-driven and data-first, but most are still gut-driven. At Orfium, we believe a company cannot claim to be any of these fancy terms if there is no proper data culture embedded in all job levels. We do not need just data literacy but a data culture altogether.

In this talk, we will define data culture and how we built it at Orfium. We will also explain how this connects self-service analytics, data cataloging, and data governance initiatives. This talk is beneficial to all data managers, including those who need to unlock time for their teams to do more advanced initiatives rather than spending time fixing formulas on sheets for non-technical departments.

Thomas Antonakis - Principal BI Engineer at Orfium
Experienced Head of Business Intelligence with demonstrated history of working in the internet industry and team-leading. Skilled in Business Planning, Analysis, Business Intelligence and Data Visualization. Strong Business Intelligence professional with a Master of Science (MSc) focused in Operations Research from Athens University of Economics and Business.

### Multi-project collaboration with dbt

dbt 1.6 will go one step further into making collaboration between different dbt projects a breeze. In this session, myself and a colleague will go over those new dbt features that will help you get the most out of working with different dbt projects while keeping full inter-operability.

Charles Verleyen - Lead architect at Astrafy
I am a 34 years old data engineer and have been working for the last 10 years in different industry before founding my company recently with a focus on providing modern data services. I am passionate about open-source technologies and strive to deliver automated data solutions that can scale.

Aside from my work, I am a sport lover with special dedication for tennis.

🗣Presentation #1: Why dbt will one day be bigger than Spark - Kris Peeters, CEO at Data Minded
In this talk, I will discuss how the data world is moving from distributed processing frameworks like Spark to more high-level tooling like dbt. It is based on a blog I wrote more than 2 years ago, and we see the transition happen today: https://medium.com/datamindedbe/why-dbt-will-one-day-be-bigger-than-spark-2225cadbdad0

Kris is the founder and CEO of Data Minded. We serve large enterprises and growing startups by designing, building and running data initiatives in the cloud.

🗣Presentation #2: Bring your own compute: benefits & pitfalls of developing analytics with DuckDB - Frederik Druyts, Consultant at Datashift
When was the last time you really needed that 16-machine cluster to quickly check an assumption about your data while writing a new model? Developers often have powerful machines sitting right on their desks, though they rarely use any of that power when doing analytics engineering.

In this session, we will explore how we can leverage DuckDB to efficiently fetch data from cloud storage and build, explore, and test a data model all from a local environment. We will discuss the benefits and pitfalls of this approach, including how it can help us reduce the cost and complexity of our analytics engineering efforts, as well as its potential limitations.

Frederik is skilled at designing, implementing and maintaining end-to-end data solutions. As an analytical mind with a heart for technology, he wants to help the right people ask the right questions and get the right answers from data they can trust. He achieves impact on the intersection of grasping business needs, mastering toolsets both time-tested and new, and writing clean SQL.

🗣Presentation #3: Speed up ML development with python models in dbt - Dorian Van den Heede, Machine Learning Engineer at Dataroots
In this talk, Dorian will share his experience on do's and don'ts of python models within dbt for ML and demonstrate a few design patterns to speed up development responsibly within dbt.
While SQL is still the preferred language within dbt, python models bring more expressive power to your transformation pipelines. Exactly what we want and need as machine learning engineers for our data preparation! You can go even further to defining your entire ML production pipeline within dbt, inference included.

Dorian (28y) is a Machine Learning Engineer at Dataroots. With his background in Computer Science Engineering and 5 years of experience at multiple clients, bringing machine learning models to production according to software best practices is what he thrives at. Aside of that, he was involved in the AI music band Beatroots, writes punk rock songs on his guitar and can be found blitzing chess moves online.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

Data Mesh with dbt (Charles Verleyen & Lukasz Sciga - Astrafy)

Data Mesh has become a powerful buzzwords and every data practitioner has heard about it. The major problem with buzzwords is that there are a lot of interpretations around them and this can generate quite some frustration. In case of data mesh, it can also slow down the adoption of this amazing new paradigm.

In this session I will demystify the main concepts of data mesh and bring those to life with dbt with concrete data products from Astrafy real data. You can expect to see data mesh in actions in a very pragmatic way as we will cover as well activation of data from those data products by downstream applications (leveraging dbt semantic layer).

Charles is a 34 years old data engineer and has been working for the last 10 years in different industries before founding his company recently with a focus on providing modern data services. He is passionate about open-source technologies and strives to deliver automated data solutions that can scale.
Aside from his work, he's a sport lover with special dedication for tennis.

Boosting Collaboration and Productivity with DevContainers and dbt Core (Mateusz Marciniewicz - dataroots)

In this presentation we will discuss the benefits of using devcontainers together with dbt Core for data modeling and transformation. DevContainers provide a consistent, isolated environment for development, ensuring that all developers have the same set of tools and dependencies installed. This makes it easier to collaborate and reduces the risk of conflicts or errors. By adding dbt Core to the devcontainer, developers can take advantage of its powerful features and leverage the capabilities of the VS Code IDE. This can greatly simplify the process of working with data and onboarding process of new employees, allowing developers to focus on creating value rather than managing infrastructure.

Mateusz is Polish-born with a background in Computer Science. He came to Belgium to pursue his studies, and he decided to stay and build his career here. In his free time, he enjoys sports and camping.

Documenting KPIs: Beyond dbt Semantic Layer (Juan Manuel Perafan - GoDataDriven/Xebia)

The dbt Semantic Layer has made a lot of teams reflect on the way they document their KPI's. But why stopping there? In this talk, I will break down all the knowledge required to understand a metric. From how it ties to your business goals, perverse incentives, to its technical implementation.

Juan's mission is to help data professionals to create reliable production-grade data workflows that are easy to maintain, have been properly tested, and are impeccably documented.
Half of the time, he teaches analysts how to apply engineering best practices to their workflows. The other half, he helps engineers to get in the shoes of the analysts.

Welcome to the first edition of our Analytics Engineering Meetup! This group is dedicated to bringing together professionals and enthusiasts interested in exploring the latest developments and trends in the field of analytics engineering. We aim to provide a platform for members to network, share ideas, and learn from each other in a relaxed and supportive environment. Whether you're a seasoned veteran or just starting out, we encourage you to come along and join the conversation. Let's make this a great success!

Schedule

17h45: welcome with food & drinks
18h30: Lessons learned in the trenches with dbt (Mathias Lavaert - DPG Media)
19h00: dbt for data scientists - how to avoid the common pitfalls? (Grégoire Hornung - Qover)

Lessons learned in the trenches with dbt (Mathias Lavaert)
I'd like to share my experience using dbt in production workflows with multiple clients. There some common pitfalls and misconceptions around dbt, but also some lesser known useful things.

Mathias has a decade of experience working on several data-intensive projects in various roles, ranging from engineer to enterprise data architect. Currently he helps DPG Media to transition to a data mesh architecture as a Principal Data Engineer. Mathias lives on the outskirts of Brussels with his wife. Together they're building a small farm specialized in breeding Herdwick sheep.

dbt for data scientists - how to avoid the common pitfalls? (Grégoire Hornung)

Many data scientists are now turning into so-called analytics engineers as the industry realizes that easy-to-access, high quality data is a prerequisite before any advanced modelling work.

While data scientists and analytics engineers are both data practitioners, the way they approach problems can slightly differ.
In this presentation, I will share my personal experience and discuss the common challenges that both data scientists / aspiring analytics engineers may encounter when building a data infrastructure with dbt.

In his talk, Grégoire will use concrete situations faced by his team at Qover, an insurtech scale-up based in Brussels. Grégoire works at Qover as head of data. Qover is a leader in embedded insurance orchestration, aiming to create a global safety net with insurance that empowers people to live life to the fullest.

How dbt Enabled OTA Insight's Seamless Migration from ChartIO to Looker (Lise Kerckhove)

During this talk, I will uncover the story of OTA Insight's journey from our initial BI tool, ChartIO, to a self-service data platform powered by Looker. You will learn how dbt was the key enabler in this migration and how it transformed OTA Insight's data strategy, delivering consistent reporting, data reliability, and empowering business stakeholders to do their own analyses.

How dbt helped avoid a vendor lock-in and reduced the migration time to less than 4 months
Best practices for testing and validating data transformations
How to create a self-service-focused data tool that serves not only your end-users but also your leadership team and board members
How OTA Insight optimized their data architecture for Looker and the impact on their data analysis process

Get ready to take your data transformation to the next level!

Lise Kerkckhove works as Team Lead Analytics Engineering at OTA Insight. From the very beginning in 2012, OTA Insight’s vision is to provide user friendly revenue management tools, as well as becoming the global leader in data intelligence for the hospitality industry.

Location & parking

⚠️⚠️⚠️ LOCATION UPDATE:
Due to unforeseen circumstances we had to change the location last-minute to De Hoorn in Leuven. It is very close by the original location.

The event will be in "Machinezaal" at De Hoorn in Leuven. The address is Sluisstraat 79, 3000 Leuven.

🅿️ You can park your car on the side of the road of the Kolonel Begaultlaan. Do not forget to put out your blue parking disc. If there is no parking space on the side of the road, you can also easily park at Parking Vaartkom.
Note: do not park in the Dreamland parking lot. This parking closes at 6.30 PM and you won’t be able to leave with your car.
